_description: Curated text dataset for AIML 2003 Module 3: Text Embeddings lab. 60 texts across 4 categories (15 each) plus 5 cross-category edge cases for failure analysis.

texts.text: The new chip uses a 3-nanometer process, which means more transistors in the same space and better power efficiency for mobile devices.
Version control saves every change you make to your code, so you can always roll back if something breaks after an update.
The phone's battery lasts two full days because the screen only refreshes at 60Hz when displaying static content.
Open-source contributors fixed a critical security vulnerability in the library within six hours of the initial report.
Cloud storage means your files exist on servers in a data center somewhere, not physically on your laptop.
The keyboard uses mechanical switches that register a keypress halfway through the travel, so you don't have to bottom out each key.
Noise-canceling headphones use tiny microphones to sample outside sound and generate an inverse wave that cancels it out.
The router broadcasts on both 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands. The lower frequency carries farther but the higher one moves data faster.
Solid-state drives have no moving parts, which makes them faster and more durable than traditional spinning hard drives.
Two-factor authentication adds a second step beyond your password, usually a code sent to your phone, to prove you are who you say you are.
The compiler translates human-readable code into machine instructions that the processor can execute directly.
E-ink displays reflect ambient light like paper instead of emitting their own, which is why e-readers are comfortable to read in direct sunlight.
Containerization packages an application with all its dependencies so it runs the same way on every machine, from a developer's laptop to a production server.
The graphics card renders frames by calculating the color of millions of pixels sixty times per second.
Bluetooth Low Energy uses a fraction of the power of classic Bluetooth, which is why fitness trackers can run for weeks on a tiny battery.
She crossed the finish line with a personal best of 10.87 seconds, shaving a full tenth off her previous record.
The goalkeeper read the penalty kick early, diving left before the striker's foot made contact with the ball.
Free throws are the only uncontested shots in basketball, and yet league-wide accuracy has hovered around 77 percent for decades.
The cycling team used a rotating pace line to share wind resistance across all eight riders over the 200-kilometer stage.
A tennis serve that lands in the corner of the service box gives the opponent less than half a second to react.
The referee awarded a red card after reviewing the tackle on the sideline monitor, overturning the original yellow.
Marathon runners hit the wall around kilometer 32 when glycogen reserves run out and the body switches to burning fat.
The pitcher's fastball registered 98 miles per hour on the stadium radar gun, but his slider was the pitch that struck out the side.
Olympic swimmers shave their entire bodies before competition because even fine hair creates measurable drag in the water.
The defensive line shifted to a 3-4 formation in the second half, which pressured the quarterback into three consecutive incomplete passes.
Rock climbers chalk their hands to absorb moisture and improve grip on small holds, especially during long routes in warm conditions.
The ice hockey team pulled their goalie with two minutes left, adding an extra skater to push for the tying goal.
A regulation soccer match is 90 minutes, but professional players only have the ball for an average of two minutes total.
Weightlifters wear flat-soled shoes because a raised heel shifts balance forward and reduces power transfer through the floor.
The rowing crew synchronized their stroke rate at 36 per minute for the final 500 meters, gaining half a boat length on the leaders.
Caramelization starts around 170 degrees Celsius, when sugar molecules break apart and reform into hundreds of new flavor compounds.
A sharp knife is safer than a dull one because it requires less force, giving you more control over each cut.
Resting a steak for five minutes after cooking lets the juices redistribute, so they stay in the meat instead of pooling on the plate.
Sourdough bread relies on wild yeast captured from the air rather than commercial packets, which is why every starter tastes slightly different.
Deglazing the pan with wine lifts the caramelized bits from the bottom, turning them into the foundation of a rich sauce.
Blind baking means pre-baking a pie crust without filling so it stays crisp when you add a wet filling later.
Searing chicken skin-side down in a cold pan lets the fat render slowly, producing a crispier result than starting in hot oil.
Kosher salt has larger, flatter crystals than table salt, which makes it easier to pinch and distribute evenly by hand.
The Maillard reaction is not caramelization. It happens between amino acids and sugars, and it is responsible for the brown crust on bread and grilled meat.
Emulsification binds oil and vinegar into a stable dressing. Mustard acts as the emulsifier by holding the two liquids together.
Mise en place means measuring and arranging every ingredient before you start cooking, which prevents scrambling mid-recipe.
Blanching vegetables in boiling water for 30 seconds and then plunging them in ice water preserves their bright color and crisp texture.
A cast-iron skillet retains heat so well that it keeps cooking food even after you remove it from the burner.
The acid in lemon juice cooks raw fish in ceviche by denaturing the proteins, changing the texture without any heat.
Bread dough needs time to rise because the yeast produces carbon dioxide gas, which gets trapped in the gluten network and creates air pockets.
The overnight train from Bangkok to Chiang Mai costs less than a budget flight and arrives in time for breakfast.
Packing cubes compress clothing into tight bundles that fit together like puzzle pieces, which is how frequent travelers fit a week into a carry-on.
Jet lag hits harder when you fly east because your body has to advance its clock, which is biologically harder than delaying it.
The hostel had a rooftop terrace overlooking the old city, and the breakfast was better than some of the restaurants we tried.
Travel insurance seems expensive until you need emergency medical evacuation from a remote island, which can cost fifty thousand dollars without coverage.
The ancient aqueduct still stands after two thousand years, its arches carrying water across the valley on the same gravity-fed principle the original engineers intended.
Currency exchange kiosks at airports charge a 10 to 15 percent markup. Withdrawing from a local ATM with a no-fee card is almost always cheaper.
The altitude in La Paz sits above 3,600 meters, so most visitors spend their first day moving slowly and drinking coca tea.
A window seat on the left side of the plane gives you the best view of the Alps on the flight from London to Rome.
The night market stretched for six blocks, with vendors selling everything from grilled skewers to handmade leather sandals.
Off-season rates at the resort were 40 percent cheaper than peak summer, and the beaches were nearly empty.
Learning even five phrases in the local language changes how people respond to you. A greeting and a thank-you go a long way.
The ferry crossing takes four hours, but the scenery along the fjord makes it feel like the highlight of the trip rather than just transportation.
Global Entry membership includes TSA PreCheck, so you skip the long security line on domestic flights and the customs line on international arrivals.
The guidebook recommended a restaurant that closed two years ago, which is why checking recent online reviews matters more than print editions.

edge_cases.text: The team's nutritionist designed a meal plan with precise macronutrient ratios to fuel the athletes through their pre-season training camp.
edge_cases.note: Nutrition bridges sports and cooking. Embeddings will likely place this closer to sports (athlete context) while TF-IDF might split on food vocabulary.

edge_cases.text: The food blog's server crashed after a viral recipe post generated ten million page views in twelve hours.
edge_cases.note: Server infrastructure meets food content. The dominant nouns pull in different directions.

edge_cases.text: GPS navigation has made it nearly impossible to get genuinely lost, which some backpackers say has taken the adventure out of travel.
edge_cases.note: Technology applied to travel. Embeddings should catch the travel sentiment; TF-IDF may weight 'GPS' and 'navigation' toward technology.

edge_cases.text: The stadium concourse served artisanal tacos and craft beer from a rotating selection of local vendors.
edge_cases.note: Food at a sporting event. Neither the sports nor the cooking angle dominates.

edge_cases.text: She documented her cycling tour through Tuscany, posting daily photos of hilltop villages and roadside trattoria meals.
edge_cases.note: Triple overlap: cycling (sports), Tuscany (travel), trattoria meals (cooking). A genuine edge case for any embedding model.
